title: Ukraine and Russia Both Agree to Orthodox Easter Truce
detail: Both Russia and Ukraine have agreed to an Orthodox Easter truce, with President Zelenskyy confirming Ukraine's acceptance — advancing beyond Putin's earlier unilateral announcement. The agreement marks a significant diplomatic development in the conflict, representing the first bilateral ceasefire commitment since the start of the full-scale invasion. Orthodox Easter falls on April 20, 2026. The mutual agreement signals a potential pause in hostilities, though details on monitoring and enforcement remain unclear.

title: Bloomberg: Ukraine and Russia Moving Toward Potential Peace Deal
detail: Bloomberg reports that Ukraine and Russia are moving toward a potential peace deal, marking significant diplomatic movement amid the ongoing Orthodox Easter truce. The development follows both sides' agreement to the Easter ceasefire and signals broader momentum toward negotiations. Key details of the prospective deal framework have not yet been disclosed, but the Bloomberg report represents a major shift from the diplomatic stalemate that characterized the conflict through much of early 2026. US and European mediators are believed to be actively involved in facilitating the talks.
